kill_fact: No kill_fact. The bear/PASS thesis survives independent verification against primary SEC filings. Every load-bearing figure checks out: Q1-2026 net sales +11% to $1.52B / organic +8.2% (8-K 04-30-2026); FY adj. EPS guide RAISED to $19.30-$19.85 (confirmed, and this is a SPENT re-rate, not a forward catalyst); NSI Industries $3.0B cash acquisition CLOSED 09-Jun-2026 funded with $900M term loan + $1.9B senior notes + commercial paper (8-K, confirming the leverage step-up the bear relies on); spot ~$490, market cap ~$25.9B, trailing PE ~28x (full valuation confirmed). The sop_fair_value (~$483, ~24.5x fwd on $19.58) is built on POST-guidance-raise, post-NSI-close financials — NOT a stale anchor. The catalyst slate is correctly characterized as SOFT_EXTENDED / already-fired. The PASS (verdict C) holds.
corrections: (1) STALE/OVERSTATED TORQUE: bull leans on 'data-center sales +40%' and an Electrical Solutions 'torque' story, but the Q1-2026 headline 8-K/press release contains NO +40% figure (call commentary only) and Electrical Solutions ADJUSTED operating margin actually CONTRACTED 30bps y/y to 16.4% — the data-center mix is margin-DILUTIVE at the segment level, mildly reinforcing the bear, not the bull. (2) Quality of earnings is in Utility Solutions (adj. margin 21.8%, +190bps), confirming the dossier's point that the high-margin durability sits in the grid book, not the data-center sleeve the multiple fixates on. Neither correction rescues asymmetry from a ~28x trailing entry. The book-independence problem (shared AI-capex factor across Basket B) is structural and unverifiable-away.
evidence: SEC 8-K exhibit 99.1, Hubbell Q1-2026 results, filed 30-Apr-2026: net sales +11% to $1.517B, organic +8.2%, adj. EPS $3.93 (+16%), FY2026 adj. EPS guide raised to $19.30-$19.85; Utility Solutions $948.9M / adj. op margin 21.8% (+190bps); Electrical Solutions $567.8M / adj. op margin 16.4% (-30bps).

SEC 8-K, Hubbell, filed ~10-Jun-2026: $3.0B NSI Industries cash acquisition CLOSED 09-Jun-2026, financed via new $900M unsecured term loan + $1.9B senior notes + commercial paper (confirms the ~$2.8B debt-funded leverage step-up).

PR Newswire, 'Sentinel Capital Partners Sells NSI Industries to Hubbell for $3.0 Billion' (2026): NSI ~15,000 SKUs / 2,000+ NA distributors, fittings/connectors/wire-management (Bridgeport, Polaris, Tork) — corroborates the bolt-on into the connector bottleneck and the deal size.
Market data, mid-June 2026: HUBB ~$489.73 spot, market cap ~$25.9B, trailing PE ~28x, div yield ~1.17% — confirms valuation is full and base-case SoP ~$483 sits ~1% below spot (no asymmetry).
